questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar belonged to which Maratha ruling house?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was the queen of the Holkar dynasty of the Maratha confederacy.

questions.q: From which capital city did Ahilyabai Holkar rule her realm?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai made Maheshwar, on the Narmada, the capital of her Holkar realm.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar ruled which central Indian region?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai governed the Malwa realm of the Holkars in central India.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar is especially renowned for funding the building and repair of what across India?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai funded temples, ghats, wells, and rest houses across India, earning lasting fame for her piety.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar ruled during which century CE?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai ruled the Holkar realm in the later 18th century CE.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar is often given which admiring nickname?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai is celebrated as the Philosopher Queen for her just and pious governance.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar was part of which larger Indian power?
questions.explanation: The Holkars under Ahilyabai were one of the great chief-houses of the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: On which sacred river does Ahilyabai's capital Maheshwar stand?
questions.explanation: Maheshwar, Ahilyabai's capital, stands on the banks of the Narmada river.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar was the daughter-in-law of which founder of the Holkar house?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was the daughter-in-law of Malhar Rao Holkar, founder of the Holkar dynasty.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar is remembered above all for what style of governance?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai is renowned for her just, compassionate, and pious governance of Malwa.

questions.q: In which year did Ahilyabai Holkar take charge of the Holkar realm and begin her long reign?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai assumed direct rule of the Holkar state in 1767 after the deaths of her husband and father-in-law.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's husband, who died before his father, was named what?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was married to Khanderao Holkar, son of Malhar Rao, who died in battle in 1754.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar's husband Khanderao died during a siege at which place in 1754?
questions.explanation: Khanderao Holkar was killed by cannon fire during the siege of Kumher fort in 1754.

questions.q: Ahilyabai relied on which trusted commander to lead her armies in the field?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai entrusted military command of the Holkar forces to her able general Tukoji Rao Holkar.

questions.q: For how long, roughly, did Ahilyabai Holkar rule the Malwa realm?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai ruled from 1767 until her death in 1795, a reign of nearly thirty years.

questions.q: In which year did Ahilyabai Holkar die?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai Holkar died in 1795 after nearly three decades of celebrated rule.

questions.q: Ahilyabai famously rebuilt which great Shiva temple at Varanasi (Kashi)?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai funded the rebuilding of the present Kashi Vishwanath Temple at Varanasi around 1780.

questions.q: Ahilyabai also sponsored a famous temple at Somnath in which present-day state?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai funded a temple at Somnath in Gujarat, one of her many religious endowments across India.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's capital Maheshwar also became renowned as a centre for the weaving of what?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai promoted a textile industry at Maheshwar, the origin of the celebrated Maheshwari sarees.

questions.q: Ahilyabai Holkar held nominal allegiance to which Maratha sovereign authority?
questions.explanation: As a Holkar chief, Ahilyabai owed nominal allegiance to the Maratha Peshwa and Chhatrapati.

questions.q: Ahilyabai funded temples and dharamshalas at pilgrimage sites as distant as which holy town in the far south?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai's endowments reached pilgrimage sites across India, including as far south as Rameswaram.

questions.q: Ahilyabai is said to have personally held public audiences (durbar) to do what?
options: Mint her own gold coins abroad
Hear and decide her subjects' grievances
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was known for holding daily public audiences to hear the grievances of ordinary subjects.

questions.q: In which year was Ahilyabai Holkar born?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was born in 1725 in the village of Chondi in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Ahilyabai was born in the village of Chondi in which present-day district region?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was born at Chondi in the Jamkhed area of the Ahmadnagar district of present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's son, who briefly held the gaddi and predeceased her, was named what?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai's son Malerao Holkar held the throne briefly in 1766-67 but died young, leading her to rule directly.

questions.q: After Ahilyabai's death in 1795, which Holkar commander formally became ruler of the state?
questions.explanation: Tukoji Rao Holkar, Ahilyabai's general, became the formal ruler of the Holkar state after her death.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's father-in-law Malhar Rao Holkar had originally risen to prominence under which Peshwa?
questions.explanation: Malhar Rao Holkar rose under Peshwa Baji Rao I, founding the Holkar fortunes in Malwa.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's principal deity, to whom she dedicated her rule and many endowments, was which form of the divine?
questions.explanation: A devout Shaiva, Ahilyabai dedicated her governance to Shiva and built and restored many Shiva temples.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's capital was moved to Maheshwar from which earlier seat of the Holkar state?
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai shifted the Holkar capital from Indore to Maheshwar on the Narmada.

questions.q: Ahilyabai's reputation for fair revenue policy meant she was noted for doing what for her cultivators?
options: Confiscating all farmland for the crown
Keeping taxes moderate and protecting peasants
questions.explanation: Ahilyabai was praised for moderate taxation and protecting the welfare of farmers and the common people.
